lead: AI continues to evolve, impacting various sectors from social media to personal relationships, while raising concerns about its implications on society and individual autonomy.

stories.title: If we’re serious about social cohesion, these big tech hate machines must be reined in
stories.summary: The Sydney Morning Herald discusses the divisive nature of social media algorithms, which are increasingly seen as harmful to social cohesion. The article argues for a need to regain control over these 'hate machines' that prioritize engagement over community well-being.

stories.title: Grindr tests AI match-making in Australia amid dating app fatigue and safety concerns
stories.summary: Grindr is introducing AI-driven matchmaking features in Australia as it faces user fatigue and safety concerns. The new subscription service aims to enhance user experience by leveraging AI to provide better matches, although it comes with a hefty price tag.

stories.title: Can the creator economy stay afloat in a flood of AI slop?
stories.summary: TechCrunch explores the challenges facing the creator economy amidst the rise of AI-generated content. The discussion centers on whether genuine creators can maintain visibility and success in an environment flooded with AI-generated material.

stories.title: AI Slop Is Destroying the Internet. These Are the People Fighting to Save It
stories.summary: CNET highlights the efforts of various individuals and organizations working to combat the negative effects of low-quality AI-generated content on the internet. This movement aims to preserve the integrity and quality of online information.

stories.title: Sam Altman would like to remind you that humans use a lot of energy, too
stories.summary: OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman addresses concerns about the energy consumption of AI technologies by comparing it to human energy usage. His remarks aim to contextualize the environmental impact of AI within broader human activities.

stories.title: I’m worried my boyfriend’s use of AI is affecting his ability to think for himself
stories.summary: An article in The Guardian discusses the potential overdependence on AI tools, as experienced by a woman concerned about her boyfriend's reliance on chatbots. This raises questions about autonomy and critical thinking in the age of AI.

stories.title: How to Hide Google’s AI Overviews From Your Search Results
stories.summary: Wired provides tips on how to avoid Google's AI-generated summaries in search results, reflecting growing user concerns over AI's influence on information access and the desire for more control over search experiences.

stories.title: China’s brain-computer interface industry is racing ahead
stories.summary: TechCrunch reports on China's rapid advancements in brain-computer interface technology, fueled by strong government support and investor interest, showcasing how AI is pushing the boundaries of human-computer interaction.
